description: You are a backend developer building a data interchange layer for the product catalog in the AdventureWorks2022 database. Implement JSON export and import capabilities by creating a stored procedure named 'dbo.usp_ExportProductCatalog' that accepts a '@CategoryName' parameter and returns a nested JSON document (Category -> Subcategories -> Products) using FOR JSON PATH by joining Production.Product, Production.ProductSubcategory, and Production.ProductCategory. Export the result for the 'Bikes' category to /home/ga/Documents/exports/product_catalog.json. You also need to create a staging table 'Production.ProductReviewStaging' with columns StagingID (INT IDENTITY PK), ProductID (INT), ReviewerName (NVARCHAR(100)), Rating (INT), Comments (NVARCHAR(MAX)), and ImportedDate (DATETIME DEFAULT GETDATE()). Implement a stored procedure 'dbo.usp_ImportProductReviews' that accepts a '@ReviewJSON' parameter, parses the array using OPENJSON, and inserts the rows into the staging table. Execute the import procedure using this JSON payload: [{"ProductID": 776, "ReviewerName": "David Ortiz", "Rating": 5, "Comments": "Excellent mountain bike, handles rough terrain perfectly."}, {"ProductID": 749, "ReviewerName": "Sarah Kim", "Rating": 4, "Comments": "Great road bike, comfortable on long rides."}, {"ProductID": 771, "ReviewerName": "Mike Torres", "Rating": 3, "Comments": "Decent bike but gears needed adjustment out of the box."}, {"ProductID": 750, "ReviewerName": "Lisa Monroe", "Rating": 5, "Comments": "Best road bike I have ever owned, superb handling."}, {"ProductID": 772, "ReviewerName": "James Reed", "Rating": 4, "Comments": "Solid build quality and well worth the price."}]
